Wait, another consideration: rider fatigue. A longer shift might lead to lower efficiency in the latter part of the day. So maybe it's better to split into two shifts or adjust start and end times to maintain peak performance during prime earning hours.

Alternatively, working a slightly shorter shift but focusing on high-demand periods could yield higher earnings without burnout.

But since I earned well yesterday with a long shift, perhaps extending it a bit more today would help capture even more orders.

In conclusion, after considering all these factors, I think starting at 8:00 AM as usual and ending at 19:00 (7 PM) could be beneficial to capture more evening orders, potentially increasing my earnings further.
